content:
Hello,

I'm trying to build the rust-for-linux kernel repository with Rust support enabled, make LLVM=1 rustavailable prints Rust is available! but when I'm executing make menuconfig or make LLVM=1 menuconfig Rust in General setup is nowhere to be found. Any ideas?

Latest revision of the rust branch
You can use / to search for the RUST symbol, there are some options that conflict with rust and if they are enabled you will not see the rust checkbox (one of them is DEBUG_INFO_BTF).

y86 said:

You can use / to search for the RUST symbol, there are some options that conflict with rust and if they are enabled you will not see the rust checkbox (one of them is DEBUG_INFO_BTF).

I managed to fix this by enabling everything hard-coded after defconfig. This is a a bit weird.

Charalampos Mitrodimas said:

make LLVM=1 rustavailable prints Rust is available! but when I'm executing make menuconfig or make LLVM=1 menuconfig Rust in General setup is nowhere to be found. Any ideas?

rustavailable only shows if the toolchain is available, not whether CONFIG_RUST is enabled or not (which, as @y86 points out, has some requirements), please see the help target and the Quick Start guide:

Rust targets:
  rustavailable   - Checks whether the Rust toolchain is
                    available and, if not, explains why.

Charalampos Mitrodimas said:

I managed to fix this by enabling everything hard-coded after defconfig. This is a a bit weird.

Do you mean modifying the .config? That is doable, but in general, you should prefer menuconfig to find requirements, read descriptions, etc.
